name: s3scanner
title: s3scanner

commands.name: s3scanner

arguments.description: string  Name of bucket to check.
arguments.raw: -bucket        string  Name of bucket to check.

arguments.description: string  File of bucket names to check.
arguments.raw: -bucket-file   string  File of bucket names to check.

arguments.description: Connect to RabbitMQ to get buckets. Requires config file key "mq". Default: "false"
arguments.raw: -mq                    Connect to RabbitMQ to get buckets. Requires config file key "mq". Default: "false"

arguments.description: Save results to a Postgres database. Requires config file key "db.uri". Default: "false"
arguments.raw: -db       Save results to a Postgres database. Requires config file key "db.uri". Default: "false"

arguments.description: Print logs to stdout in JSON format instead of human-readable. Default: "false"
arguments.raw: -json     Print logs to stdout in JSON format instead of human-readable. Default: "false"

arguments.description: Enumerate bucket objects (can be time-consuming). Default: "false"
arguments.raw: -enumerate           Enumerate bucket objects (can be time-consuming). Default: "false"

arguments.description: string  Object storage provider: aws, custom, digitalocean, dreamhost, gcp, linode - custom requires config file. Default: "aws"
arguments.raw: -provider    string  Object storage provider: aws, custom, digitalocean, dreamhost, gcp, linode - custom requires config file. Default: "aws"

arguments.description: int     Number of threads to scan with. Default: "4"
arguments.raw: -threads     int     Number of threads to scan with. Default: "4"

commands.raw_help:
root@kali:~# s3scanner -h
 INPUT: (1 required)
 -bucket        string  Name of bucket to check.
 -bucket-file   string  File of bucket names to check.
 -mq                    Connect to RabbitMQ to get buckets. Requires config file key "mq". Default: "false"
 
 OUTPUT:
 -db       Save results to a Postgres database. Requires config file key "db.uri". Default: "false"
 -json     Print logs to stdout in JSON format instead of human-readable. Default: "false"
 
 OPTIONS:
 -enumerate           Enumerate bucket objects (can be time-consuming). Default: "false"
 -provider    string  Object storage provider: aws, custom, digitalocean, dreamhost, gcp, linode - custom requires config file. Default: "aws"
 -threads     int     Number of threads to scan with. Default: "4"
 
 DEBUG:
 -verbose     Enable verbose logging. Default: "false"
 -version     Print version Default: "false"
 
 If config file is required these locations will be searched for config.yml: "." "/etc/s3scanner/" "$HOME/.s3scanner/"

binary_metadata.file_type: ELF 64-bit LSB executable, x86-64, version 1 (SYSV), dynamically linked, interpreter /lib64/ld-linux-x86-64.so.2, BuildID[sha1]=0c341b3974e00d2feadd26454eec7a1ffe4d8ec5, for GNU/Linux 3.2.0, stripped

apt_metadata.description:
tool to find open S3 buckets and dump their contents
This package contains a tool to find open S3 buckets and dump their contents.
The features are:
* zap Multi-threaded scanning
* telescope Supports tons of S3-compatible APIs
* female_detective Scans all bucket permissions to find misconfigurations
* floppy_disk Dump bucket contents to a local folder
* whale Docker support

use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use s3scanner because ### s3scanner.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair s3scanner with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `s3scanner --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.

documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'any' phase against surfaces [web] in the Kali Linux category. Fits when the engagement needs: ### s3scanner.
usage_sections: Install with: sudo apt install s3scanner
Install with: sudo apt install s3scanner
